company_type: Food Manufacturing - Spices, Seasonings, and Flavorful Products

supply_chain.dependencies: Most significant raw materials are dairy products, pepper, onion, garlic, capsicums (red peppers and paprika), tomato products, sugar and salts. Pepper and other spices and herbs are generally sourced from countries other than the U.S.
supply_chain.risks: Raw materials subject to fluctuations in market price and availability caused by weather, growing and harvesting conditions, climate change, market conditions, including inflationary cost increases, and other factors beyond control. Reduced availability of transportation capacity due to labor shortages and higher fuel costs. Reliance on third-party transportation providers.

regulatory_risks: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act compliance
Customs and trade laws, including import and export of products and product ingredients
Privacy laws and data protection (GDPR, CCPA, CPRA)
Environmental protection laws (federal, state, and local)
Proposition 65 (California Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act)
Enforcement of existing laws and changes in legal requirements
Climate change regulations (greenhouse gas emissions, carbon pricing, cap and trade systems)
ESG disclosure rules and sustainability standards
Tariffs, quotas, trade barriers and similar restrictions
Changes in tax laws and interpretations

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions exposure (exited consumer business in Russia in 2022)
Potential broader economic disruption from ongoing conflicts between Russia and Ukraine
War in the Middle East impacts
Rising tensions between China and Taiwan
Trade restrictions and embargoes affecting ability to sell, ship products, collect payments in certain regions
